name: 06_Sentiment_Analysis_SVM_Sentiment140_ML
query: Por favor, ayúdame a construir un sistema para el análisis de sentimientos en tweets utilizando el conjunto de datos Sentiment140 disponible en Hugging Face. Carga el conjunto de datos Sentiment140 y, al cargar los datos, límpialos eliminando palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`. Usa Word2Vec o GloVe para la vectorización de texto. Esto debe ocurrir en `src/data_loader.py`. Luego, implementa y entrena un clasificador SVM en `src/model.py`. Finalmente, escribe un informe de la precisión del clasificador en `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`. Idealmente, el informe debe ser fácilmente interpretable.

requirements.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Sentiment140", disponible en "Hugging Face", se obtiene en `src/data_loader.py`.
El conjunto de datos se limpia, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`.
Se utilizan incrustaciones de palabras, ya sea "Word2Vec" o "GloVe", para convertir texto en vectores en `src/data_loader.py`.
Un "clasificador SVM" se implementa y entrena en `src/model.py`.
La puntuación de precisión se imprime y guarda como `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.

preferences.criteria: El proceso de descarga del conjunto de datos debe ser confiable, con un manejo claro de errores.
El informe final de precisión debe ser directo y fácil de interpretar.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Sentiment140", disponible en "Hugging Face", se obtiene en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file is a minimal placeholder implementation that does not actually load the Sentiment140 dataset from Hugging Face. The `load_dataset()` function simply returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` with no actual dataset loading logic. There is no import of `datasets` from Hugging Face, no call to `load_dataset("sentiment140")` or any equivalent, and no real data retrieval mechanism. The file merely lists the requirement criteria as strings and keywords, serving as a stub rather than a functional implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos se limpia, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file is a minimal placeholder implementation that does not contain any actual data cleaning logic. It only defines a `load_dataset()` function that returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and a `build_transforms()` function returning a static list. There is no code for removing stop words, punctuation, or special characters. The file explicitly states it is a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" and contains no real preprocessing pipeline.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utilizan incrustaciones de palabras, ya sea "Word2Vec" o "GloVe", para convertir texto en vectores en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file is a minimal placeholder implementation that only lists Word2Vec and GloVe as keywords in a `KEYWORDS` list and mentions them in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` strings. There is no actual implementation of word embeddings — no imports of gensim, fasttext, or any GloVe loading utilities, no model training or loading code, and no function that converts text to vectors using Word2Vec or GloVe. The `load_dataset()` function simply returns a dictionary with a placeholder string, and `build_transforms()` returns a hardcoded list unrelated to text vectorization.

judge_stats.criteria: Un "clasificador SVM" se implementa y entrena en `src/model.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/model.py` file does not implement or train an SVM classifier. Instead, it contains only a placeholder/stub implementation with metadata strings listing "SVM classifier" as a keyword, and a `build_model()` function that returns a string placeholder ("model placeholder"). There is no actual SVM implementation (e.g., no `sklearn.svm.SVC`, `LinearSVC`, or any equivalent), no training logic, no feature extraction, and no data fitting. The file is explicitly described as a "minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging," confirming it is not a real implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: La puntuación de precisión se imprime y guarda como `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt` exists, but its content is `('placeholder\r\n', None)`, which is clearly a placeholder value rather than an actual accuracy score computed from a trained SVM model on the Sentiment140 dataset. There is no evidence of a real accuracy metric being calculated or saved. The content indicates the implementation was not completed properly.
